title: Translate this passage：(4) In 1990, the policy goal was again enhanced,

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Translate this passage：(4) In 1990, the policy goal was again enhanced,
being defined as ‘‘improving, or at least restoring’’ (instead of the previous wording
‘‘restore and if possible improve’’) the
income and livelihoods levels of resettlers
(World Bank 1990).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: 在1990年，政策目标再次得到加强，被定义为“提高，或至少恢复”（而不是先前的措辞“恢复并在可能的情况下改善”）重新安置者的收入和生计水平（世界银行1990年）。

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Translate this passage：Two elements appear clearly: First, the resettlement policy goal was never defined simply
as paying compensation, but as a complex
socio-economic reconstruction process. Second,
there has been a gradual elevation of
the objective to be achieved in resettlement.
Yet despite this raising of the bar through
several policy stages from 1980 until 2000,
there has been no corresponding change in the
financial instruments mandated to support
the increasing policy goals. The array of
financial means mobilised to implement the
more exacting policy objectives has remained
the same. An insufficiently financed mandate
was created.
To sum up, the justification for investment
as part of the financing of each resettlement
component over and above compensation rests
on three solid grounds. First, on economic
grounds, forced displacement requires enabling
those displaced to secure an accelerated path to
catch up for forgone growth. Second, on policy
consistency grounds, there is a deep mismatch
inside many formal resettlement policies between high goals and insufficient means. The
means of compensation are not commensurate
with the goals of restoration with redevelopment. Third, on poverty reduction grounds, it is
unacceptable to allow new poverty to creep in
under projects aimed at reducing existing
poverty.
The policy framework for investing in
resettlement and broadening the economic foundations of resettlement operations is gradually
strengthening. For instance, the revised resettlement policy issued by the World Bank in
November 2001 states unambiguously that ‘‘resettlement activities should be conceived and
executed as sustainable development programmes, providing sufficient investment resources to give the persons displaced by
the project the opportunity to share in project
benefits’’ (World Bank 2001, my emphasis).
There is, however, still very little in this rewritten
policy document about the procedures and
measures through which such investments and
sharing in project benefits are to be allocated
and implemented. This now awaits explicit
specification.
Important building blocks are already
available for conceptualising the new economic
foundations for resettlement operations. For
instance, much can be learned from progress in
environmental economics, from other domains
of development economics, from the vast
research on poverty reduction and social protection, and from the (still few) direct studies on
resettlement economics (Downing 2002; Kanbur
2003; Pearce 1999; Shi and Hu 1994). The vast
and growing store of research in anthropology
and sociology on the economics of displacement,
on risks, and on secondary effects, provides a
rich source of empirical data, knowledge, and
theory. In short, the challenge to economists,
sociologists and anthropologists – to articulate
jointly a new economics of resettlement – is not
only important and overdue, but can now be met
successfully

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
两个元素显然出现了：首先，重新安置政策目标从未仅被定义为支付赔偿金，而是一个复杂的社会经济重建过程。其次，重新安置中要实现的目标已经逐渐提高。但尽管从1980年到2000年通过几个政策阶段提高了目标，但支持日益增长的政策目标的金融工具并没有相应的变化。用来实现更高标准政策目标的各种财政手段保持不变。这就导致了任务资金不充足。
总之，每个重新安置组成部分资金投资并在赔偿之上的正当性判断有三个坚实的基础。首先，从经济上讲，强制性移民需要使流离失所的人获得加速赶上之前落后的增长路径的能力。其次，出于政策一致性的考虑，在许多正式重新安置政策中，高目标与不足的手段之间存在深刻的不匹配。赔偿的手段不符合恢复和发展的目标。第三，出于减贫的考虑，在旨在减少现有贫困的项目中允许新贫困的出现是不可接受的。
投资重新安置和扩大重新安置操作的经济基础的政策框架正在逐渐加强。例如，世界银行在2001年11月发布的修订版重新安置政策明确指出，“重新安置活动应该被构思和执行为可持续的发展计划，提供足够的投资资源，使项目受影响的人有机会分享项目的收益”（世界银行，2001年，我作为重点）。然而，在这个重新编写的政策文件中，关于如何分配和实施这样的投资和分享项目收益的程序和措施还是很少。这需要明确的规定。
对于构想重新安置操作新的经济基础已经有了重要的基石。例如，可以从环境经济学的进展中学到很多东西，从其他发展经济学领域中学到很多东西，从广泛的减贫和社会保护研究中学到很多东西，从关于重新安置经济学的（仍然很少）直接研究中学到很多东西（多宁，2002;康伯，2003;皮尔斯，1999;士和胡，1994）。人类学和社会学中关于流离失所的经济学、风险和二级影响的研究提供了丰富的实证数据、知识和理论。总之，经济学家、社会学家和人类学家的挑战——共同阐述一种新的重新安置经济学——不仅重要而且已经过期，现在可以成功应对。
